description:
You are a Linux systems engineer and have been asked to harden some legacy
    service configuration files that live in the user’s home directory.

    CURRENT FILE LAYOUT
    -------------------
    1. /home/user/legacy_services/nginx.conf.old
    2. /home/user/legacy_services/sshd_config.old
    3. /home/user/legacy_services/httpd.conf.old
    4. /home/user/legacy_services/hardened/          (already exists, but is empty)
    5. /home/user/hardening/                         (already exists, but is empty)

    HARDENING REQUIREMENTS
    ----------------------
    A. /home/user/legacy_services/nginx.conf.old
       • Change:  server_tokens on;      →  server_tokens off;
       • Change:  ssl_protocols TLSv1 TLSv1.1 TLSv1.2;  
                   →  ssl_protocols TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3;

    B. /home/user/legacy_services/sshd_config.old
       • Change:  PermitRootLogin yes               →  PermitRootLogin no
       • Change:  PasswordAuthentication yes        →  PasswordAuthentication no

    C. /home/user/legacy_services/httpd.conf.old
       • Change:  ServerSignature On                →  ServerSignature Off
       • Change:  TraceEnable On                    →  TraceEnable Off

    WHAT YOU MUST DO
    ----------------
    1. Create hardened copies of each file inside
       /home/user/legacy_services/hardened/  keeping the same base‐name but
       **without** the “.old” extension, e.g. the hardened nginx file must be
       /home/user/legacy_services/hardened/nginx.conf

    2. Apply the exact directive/value changes listed above and leave the rest
       of each file untouched (order and spacing of other lines must be
       preserved).  The six directives must appear exactly once each in the new
       files with their hardened values.

    3. Produce a log file at /home/user/hardening/hardening.log that lists every
       change you made.  The automated tests will read this log, so make sure
       the format is **exactly** the following, one line per change, no extra
       whitespace, no header lines, and appear in the order shown below:

       nginx.conf: server_tokens=on -> off
       nginx.conf: ssl_protocols=TLSv1 TLSv1.1 TLSv1.2 -> TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3
       sshd_config: PermitRootLogin=yes -> no
       sshd_config: PasswordAuthentication=yes -> no
       httpd.conf: ServerSignature=On -> Off
       httpd.conf: TraceEnable=On -> Off

       • File names use just the base name (no path, no “.old”).
       • Directive names are case-sensitive and must match exactly.
       • The “old” and “new” values must appear exactly as shown.

    4. Run the transformation for the three files in parallel (e.g. with “&”,
       background subshells, GNU parallel, or xargs -P).  The test harness will
       not inspect how you achieved parallelism, only that you did not block on
       each file sequentially—so use at least one explicit parallelisation
       operator or tool in your final command sequence.

    5. When everything is finished, the directory tree must look like:

       /home/user/legacy_services/hardened/nginx.conf       (hardened content)
       /home/user/legacy_services/hardened/sshd_config      (hardened content)
       /home/user/legacy_services/hardened/httpd.conf       (hardened content)
       /home/user/hardening/hardening.log                   (exact contents above)

    The automated grading script will open the three hardened files and compare
    them byte-for-byte against the expected reference versions, and then parse
    /home/user/hardening/hardening.log to ensure each of the six lines matches
    exactly.

    You do not need root.  Do everything as the default user, entirely from the
    shell prompt.  You may create temporary scripts if you wish, but they are
    not required for grading.

    NOTE
    ----
    • Do not rename or delete the original “.old” files.
    • Do not change any directives other than the six specified.
    • Ensure the log file path and the hardened directory path are exactly as
      stated—spelling matters.
truth:
Initial files (already present when the agent starts):

    /home/user/legacy_services/nginx.conf.old
    ------------------------------------------------
    user www-data;
    worker_processes auto;
    server_tokens on;
    ssl_protocols TLSv1 TLSv1.1 TLSv1.2;

    /home/user/legacy_services/sshd_config.old
    ------------------------------------------------
    # SSH Daemon Configuration
    PermitRootLogin yes
    PasswordAuthentication yes

    /home/user/legacy_services/httpd.conf.old
    ------------------------------------------------
    # Apache HTTPD Configuration
    ServerSignature On
    TraceEnable On


    Expected files after successful completion:

    /home/user/legacy_services/hardened/nginx.conf
    ------------------------------------------------
    user www-data;
    worker_processes auto;
    server_tokens off;
    ssl_protocols TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3;

    /home/user/legacy_services/hardened/sshd_config
    ------------------------------------------------
    # SSH Daemon Configuration
    PermitRootLogin no
    PasswordAuthentication no

    /home/user/legacy_services/hardened/httpd.conf
    ------------------------------------------------
    # Apache HTTPD Configuration
    ServerSignature Off
    TraceEnable Off


    Expected log file:

    /home/user/hardening/hardening.log
    ------------------------------------------------
    nginx.conf: server_tokens=on -> off
    nginx.conf: ssl_protocols=TLSv1 TLSv1.1 TLSv1.2 -> TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3
    sshd_config: PermitRootLogin=yes -> no
    sshd_config: PasswordAuthentication=yes -> no
    httpd.conf: ServerSignature=On -> Off
    httpd.conf: TraceEnable=On -> Off
